items.title: Appropriate $256,500 Mass Save Community First Partnership Grant for energy-efficiency outreach
items.summary: The City Manager requested an appropriation of $256,500 from a Mass Save Community First Partnership Grant to the Office of Sustainability. The funds will pay for outreach contractors to help residents and small businesses access energy-efficiency programs. Adopted unanimously 9-0.
items.body: Transmitting a Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of the Mass Save Community First Partnership Grant in the amount of $256,500, to the Grant Fund Office of Sustainability Other Ordinary Maintenance Account. items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75; $25 for low-income; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars/household
items.summary: Tabled carryover policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, $25 for residents in affordable housing, on SNAP, or low-income; removal of the senior exemption; and reducing the per-household cap from four permits to two. Remained on the table at the March 9 meeting; the Final Actions notes No Action Taken in Council March 16, 2026.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026. NO ACTION TAKEN IN COUNCIL MARCH 16, 2026. POR 2026-32

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

items.title: Amend Zoning Ordinance Section 4.50: religious, educational, and child-care uses as-of-right citywide
items.summary: Unfinished business amending Cambridge Zoning Ordinance Section 4.50 to allow religious, qualifying educational, and child-care uses (centers, school-age programs, family child care homes, and large family child care homes per MGL c.15D §1A) as-of-right in every zoning district. The item passed to a second reading on March 2, 2026; no action was taken at the March 9 meeting. The Final Actions also notes No Action Taken in Council March 16, 2026.
items.body: AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE AS FOLLOWS WITH THE INTENT OF PERMITTING THE USE OF LAND FOR THE FOLLOWING PURPOSES AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS: RELIGIOUS PURPOSES; EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ON LAND OWNED OR LEASED BY THE COMMONWEALTH OR ANY OF ITS AGENCIES, SUBDIVISIONS OR BODIES POLITIC OR BY A RELIGIOUS SECT OR DENOMINATION, OR BY A NONPROFIT EDUCATIONAL CORPORATION; AND FOR A CHILD CARE CENTER, SCHOOL-AGED CHILD CARE PROGRAM, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME, OR LARGE FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME AS DEFINED IN SECTION 1A OF CHAPTER 15D OF MASSACHUSETTS GENERAL LAWS. PASS TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026. NO ACTION TAKEN IN COUNCIL MARCH 16, 2026. ORD 2026-01

items.title: Open city-owned parking lots (incl. Tobin/Vassal school complex) for after-hours resident use
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the City Manager work with the School Department, DPW, and other departments to make city-owned parking lots — including the still-under-construction Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex lot — available to residents after hours.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29. Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-02

items.title: Review legal and oversight framework for Tourism Destination Marketing District and visitor information kiosk
items.summary: Awaiting-report request asking the City Solicitor and Community Development Department to report on legal parameters around City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and on oversight and accountability for the visitor information kiosk.

items.title: Prioritize 2026 pedestrianization pilots, including a Brattle Street section with two-way JFK Street
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the City Manager prioritize 2026 pedestrianization pilot projects without a consultant study, and consider additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street to enhance Harvard Square.
items.body: That the City Manager work with relevant City departments to prioritize pilot projects in 2026 without the use of a consultant for an extensive pedestrianization study, and in the medium-term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street could enhance the Square. Councillor Nolan, Councillor Simmons, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Zusy. AR 2026-12

items.title: Comprehensive review of safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the City Manager conduct a comprehensive review of safety and security at Cambridge City Hall, including physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security staffing and training, emergency-response protocols, and surveillance/alarm technology.
items.body: That the City Manager conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring. Councillor Flaherty, Councillor Simmons. AR 2026-14

items.title: Examine preventing CPD from hiring former ICE, HSI, or CBP officers
items.summary: Awaiting-report request asking the City Manager to examine if and how Cambridge can prevent the Cambridge Police Department from hiring sworn officers who previously worked for ICE, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the relevant City staff and departments to examine if and how the City may prevent the hiring by the Cambridge Police Department of any sworn officer who was hired by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol, and report back to the City Council in a timely manner. Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Al-Zubi. AR 2026-15

items.title: Examine permitting third-party guarantors / co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the Community Development Department examine the feasibility of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants and recommend whether the policy could responsibly expand access to those units.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units. Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor Simmons. AR 2026-16

items.title: Draft ordinance restricting use of City property/personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the Law Department draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement, and evaluate whether to amend the Welcoming Community Ordinance.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for the purposes of federal civil immigration enforcement, and that the Law Department evaluate whether these protections should be adopted as an amendment to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Nolan, Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor Zusy, Councillor Al-Zubi. AR 2026-17

items.title: Update digital-equity evaluation and report on internet access programs by June 2026
items.summary: Awaiting-report request that the City Manager review prior digital-equity work and steps taken since, evaluate whether residents' internet needs are being met, and report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs. Councillor Zusy, Councillor Nolan, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Vice Mayor Azeem. AR 2026-18
